_meta.description: Sample data for the Map & Spots section. Includes Norwegian dive spots, dive reports, and parking locations.
notes: spot-1 (Langøyene) has a fresh report — normal visibility display
spot-2 (Gressholmen) has a stale report (> 30 days old) — triggers amber date indicator
spot-3 (Bleikøya) has no reports — triggers 'No data yet' empty state
spot-4 (Ryvarden) and spot-5 (Ålesund Dykkesenter) are west coast spots with multiple reports
spot-6 (Lindøya) is a nearby Oslo island spot with a mid-range history
currentUserRating: spot-1 and spot-4 are rated by the current user (4 and 5); all others are null
Ratings come from SpotRating entities, not DiveReports — averageRating and currentUserRating are pre-computed aggregates

spots.name: Langøyene
spots.description: One of Oslo's most popular dive spots — shallow sandy approaches, eelgrass beds, and a gentle slope down to 18m. Great visibility after calm periods. The ferry from Aker Brygge runs in summer.
spots.accessInfo: Take the ferry from Aker Brygge (line 94). No diving equipment rental on the island. Parking at Aker Brygge P-hus.

photos.caption: Sandy bottom near the ferry dock

spots.name: Gressholmen
spots.description: A small nature reserve island with an old airport runway pier. The east side has dramatic drop-offs and good macro life. Best dived at slack tide. Accessible by boat only.
spots.accessInfo: Accessible by private boat or kayak only. No ferry service. Anchor in the cove on the north side.

spots.name: Bleikøya
spots.description: Quiet island south of Gressholmen. Forested shoreline, rocky entry points, and kelp forests starting at 4m. Rarely crowded. Depths to 22m on the west side.
spots.accessInfo: Private boat access only. Small natural harbour on the south end is suitable for anchoring.

spots.name: Ryvarden
spots.description: Rocky headland on the Hordaland coast with strong current channels and exceptional macro life. Nudibranchs, crabs, and the occasional wolf fish. Depths to 30m+ on the outer reef.
spots.accessInfo: Park at Ryvarden Fyr (lighthouse). 10-minute walk to the dive entry point on the south side. Slippery rocks — wear appropriate footwear.

photos.caption: Current channel at the outer reef

spots.name: Ålesund Dykkesenter
spots.description: Shore dive from the dive centre dock in the Borgundfjord. Sheltered from wind, easy entry, good training site. Artificial reef structures added in 2023. Max depth 14m from the dock.
spots.accessInfo: Park at Ålesund Dykkesenter. Entry fee for non-members. Gear rental available on site. Shore entry from the floating dock.

spots.name: Lindøya
spots.description: Popular summer island near Oslo with a protected cove on the north side. Sandy-bottom dive to 12m, often with good visibility. Busy in summer weekends — best dived on weekday mornings.
spots.accessInfo: Ferry from Aker Brygge (line 92). No facilities on the island. Bring all your own equipment.

reports.notes: Calm conditions after two days without wind. Visibility significantly better than last visit. Eelgrass is thick right now.
Perfect morning session. Water was calm, ferry hadn't started yet. Saw a juvenile flatfish at 8m.
Some current due to recent wind. Still enjoyable. The sandy slope is beautiful.
Excellent early-season dive. Water cold but crystal clear.
Strong current on the east side. Visibility was poor after the storm last week. The drop-off is impressive though.
Slack tide, perfect. The macro life on the east wall is incredible — three different nudibranch species.
Best west coast diving I've done in years. Water was gin clear. Two wolf fish sightings and a massive spider crab at 24m.
Strong current on the outer reef but manageable. Visibility stunning. Bring a surface marker — boats pass regularly.
Solid dive. Not quite as clear as the February visit but still great. Kelp forest on the south side is in great shape.
Good beginner spot. The artificial reef adds some structure. Nothing too exciting but solid training.
Easy entry, calm water. Perfect for working on technique. Saw a small wolf fish under the dock.
Quiet weekday morning — had the cove to myself. Sandy bottom was undisturbed, great viz.

parkingLocations.description: Aker Brygge P-hus — paid parking, 5 min walk to ferry terminal
Nesoddtangen boat launch — free parking for registered boaters
Ryvarden Fyr parking area — free, limited spaces. 10 min walk to entry point
Ålesund Dykkesenter main lot — free for members, 50 NOK/day for non-members
Street parking on Borgundvegen — free, 2-hour limit on weekdays
